name: Surendra Kumar AR
title: GenAI / ML Infra Architect · Platform Engineering · DevOps & SRE
subtitle: 11 years building platforms at scale — Walmart, Adobe, IBM — now specialising in GenAI & ML infrastructure
targeting: Dubai, UAE — GenAI / ML Infra Architect, Staff Platform Engineer roles
bio: GenAI / ML Infrastructure Architect and Platform Engineer with 11 years of experience building and operating large-scale platforms for global enterprises. Currently leading CI/CD and Developer Experience at Walmart (20,000+ engineers). Applying a deep platform engineering and SRE foundation to GenAI and ML infrastructure — MLOps pipelines, model serving, AI-powered operations, and multi-cloud ML platform architecture.

bullets: Owned the DevOps and Developer Experience platform for 20,000+ engineers globally across CI/CD, artifact management, observability, and security guardrails on AWS, Azure, GCP and On-Prem
Architected CI/CD systems handling 65,000+ artifact downloads per minute with global edge distribution (AWS, Azure, GCP — US, UK, CA) reducing build and deployment latency by ~60%
Re-architected Jenkins and ArgoCD from imperative patching to a fully GitOps declarative model with round-robin controller sharding, Auto-Scalar, and CASC — eliminating configuration drift
Automated disaster recovery for EKS, AKS and ArgoCD using Velero + Ansible-Helm, reducing RTO from ~4 hours to ~7 minutes for critical workloads
Served as Incident Commander for high-severity CI/CD platform incidents; built AI-powered CI remediation reducing recurring incident classes and on-call load
Achieved ~35% infrastructure cost reduction through autoscaling, node right-sizing, and idle environment scale-down across EKS, AKS, GKE without SLA impact
Owned modernisation of Adobe's AEM delivery platform via an OpenShift-based AEM Kubernetes Operator, encoding operational knowledge directly into the platform
Designed Kubernetes-first CI/CD architecture on Red Hat OpenShift on AWS and IBM Cloud for AEM workloads, eliminating environment-specific drift
Introduced on-demand environment provisioning with Terraform and Ansible for ROKS, replacing ticket-driven workflows with self-service automation
Drove ~40% reduction in non-production cloud costs through provisioning standardisation, right-sizing, and automated cleanup of idle environments

experience.company: IBM — Bendigo Bank, QBE Insurance, Westpac Bank
bullets: Led DevX and XOps platform transformation for large fintech clients across CI/CD, Cloud, and Kubernetes modernisation using DevOps Maturity Assessments
Identified systemic resilience gaps in Westpac & Bendigo Bank CI/CD ecosystems and led redesign — delivering a GitLab HA/DR platform with standardised pipeline templates
Designed multi-cloud platform architectures (AWS, IBM Cloud, on-prem) balancing regulatory constraints, delivery velocity, and operational resilience with DevSecOps principles
Partnered with client engineering leadership to execute platform modernisation with zero disruption to business continuity
Designed and scaled enterprise CI/CD platforms across hybrid cloud environments for regulated clients in financial services, aviation, and hospitality
Built reusable automation frameworks and self-service templates enabling 400+ teams to self-onboard applications at scale
Led container and Kubernetes platform adoption establishing lifecycle management, deployment standards, and operational guardrails
Integrated enterprise-grade security controls — IAM, RBAC, directory integrations, certificate management, and compliance guardrails
